output.title: Landlord Withholding Your Security Deposit? Copy This 3-Email Demand Sequence
output.meta_description: Is your landlord withholding your security deposit? Learn your tenant rights and use this copy-paste email sequence to demand your deposit back fast.
content_outline: Hook: The real cost when your landlord is not returning your deposit
Know Your Rights: What a landlord can and cannot deduct from your security deposit
Email 1: Initial Demand — Subject line and body copy for a polite but firm first notice
Email 2: Escalation — Statute-backed follow-up when your deposit is still not returned
Email 3: Final Notice — Ultimatum security deposit dispute letter before small claims court
State Law Cheat Sheet: How long your landlord has to return your deposit
What to do next if your landlord refuses to give your deposit back
key_points: Email 1 Subject: 'Formal Demand: Return of Security Deposit – [Rental Address]' — Body restates lease end date, demands the full deposit amount, and cites the state-mandated deadline for return.

Email 2 Subject: 'Second Notice: Unreturned Security Deposit & Itemized Deduction Request' — Body challenges improper deductions, references specific tenant rights statutes, and sets a strict 7-day response deadline.

Email 3 Subject: 'Final Notice Before Legal Action – Security Deposit Dispute for [Address]' — Body demands immediate repayment, references statutory penalties and interest, and warns of imminent small claims court filing.
Landlords may only withhold deposits for actual damages beyond normal wear and tear or documented unpaid rent, and must provide an itemized statement in most states.
A written security deposit dispute letter creates a court-admissible paper trail and often forces compliance before litigation.
output.cta: Skip the guesswork. Use DepositBack to generate a state-specific security deposit demand letter in minutes and get your money back faster.
output.target_audience: Frustrated US renters whose landlord is withholding or refusing to return a security deposit and need actionable, legally sound demand letters immediately
